title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: March 04, 2026

sections.title: Market Pulse: Geopolitical Tensions Weigh Heavily on Global Equities

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Decline Amid Escalating Middle East Conflict and Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. equities closed lower on March 4, 2026, as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East intensified, leading to a significant sell-off in global markets. All major U.S. indices trimmed earlier steep losses but still finished in negative territory. Crude oil prices surged, fueling concerns over inflation and potential economic slowdown. Optimism was slightly restored after President Donald Trump indicated the U.S. Navy could escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.statContext: Closed down 0.9% (after falling as much as 2.5% intraday)
Closed down 0.8% (after plunging over 1,200 points intraday)
Nasdaq 100 futures down 0.95%, Dow Jones futures down 0.54%, S&P 500 futures down 0.69% at 1:24 a.m. EST

items.headline: Target Rises on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Target rose significantly after reporting a better profit for the latest quarter than analysts expected.

items.headline: Palantir Benefits from Defense Spending
items.summary: Palantir Technologies gained as renewed focus on defense and intelligence spending boosted the stock. The company derives roughly half its revenue from government contracts.

items.headline: IBM Among Dow's Strongest Performers
items.summary: IBM was one of the strongest performers within the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

items.headline: Caterpillar Leads Dow's Decline
items.summary: Caterpillar led the decline for the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

items.headline: Nike Experiences Notable Drop
items.summary: Nike was among the significant decliners.

items.headline: Lithium Stocks Plunge, Led by Albemarle
items.summary: Albemarle led a broad selloff in lithium stocks following weaker demand expectations, with lithium carbonate futures dropping approximately 13%.

items.headline: U.S. Economy Poised for Reacceleration in 2026
items.summary: The spring 2026 UCLA Anderson Forecast suggests a meaningful shift in economic momentum, with the U.S. economy expected to reaccelerate. This growth is anticipated to be fueled by income tax cuts, expansion of fiscal stimulus, and sustained investment in artificial intelligence. Real GDP is projected to grow around 2.5% this year.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Drives Oil Prices Higher, Shifts Market Sentiment
items.summary: The escalating conflict in the Middle East continues to impact global markets, with Brent crude briefly topping $84 per barrel before settling at $81.40, and U.S. crude rising to $74.56. Concerns about supply disruptions, particularly after reports of Iran taking control of the Strait of Hormuz, have intensified fears of inflation and slower economic growth. Energy and defense sectors have seen surges, while airline stocks have plunged.

items.headline: Fed Official Anticipates Stable Growth and Easing Inflation
items.summary: John C. Williams, President of the New York Fed, expects real GDP growth of about 2.5% this year, a slight decrease in the unemployment rate, and inflation around 2.5% in 2026, dropping to 2% in 2027. He noted the FOMC has lowered the federal funds rate by 1.75 percentage points.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports from Abercrombie & Fitch, Broadcom, and Okta Today
items.summary: Several major companies are scheduled to report earnings today, March 4, 2026, including Abercrombie & Fitch ($ANF), Broadcom ($AVGO), Rigetti ($RGTI), and Okta ($OKTA). Investors will be closely monitoring these reports for financial updates and forward guidance.

items.headline: ADP Private Payrolls Report Expected to Show Increased Job Growth
items.summary: Traders are awaiting the release of the ADP private payrolls report. Forecasts indicate an addition of 48,000 jobs in February, an increase from 22,000 in January, suggesting continued, albeit uneven, recovery in the labor market.
